problem: Run 4 of library comments CLI implementation (Phase 2b). Prior runs built the complete CLI module (7 new files, 2 modified). All prior issues resolved (rating optional for replies via conditional require). This run focuses on hardening and verification.

files.why: Module router (73 lines). Dispatches to list, show, comments subcommands. Handles help display and item reference extraction.
List command (49 lines). Fetches and formats library items as table with ID, title, status, date.
Show command (66 lines). Displays report with section headings annotated with [slug] and comment summaries.
Comments nested router (51 lines). Dispatches to list/post subcommands.
Comments list command (80 lines). Groups comments by section anchor. Shows threading with reply indentation.
Comments post command (72 lines). RATING_MAP with aliases (up, down, love). Rating optional for --reply-to (conditional require). Auto-slugifies heading text.
Item resolution utility (82 lines). 3-format matching: cuid, ticket short ID (case-insensitive), title substring with disambiguation.
Modified: Added 'library' case to switch dispatcher at lines 94-97.
Modified: Library section in Available Commands table (lines 48-51) and Library Reports workflow section (lines 146-174) with full command examples.

facts: All 7 new files and 2 modified files exist and are complete
Item resolution supports 3 formats: cuid, ticket short ID (/^[A-Z]+-\d+$/i), title substring with disambiguation
--section flag accepts both raw slugs and heading text (auto-slugified if contains spaces)
RATING_MAP provides aliases: thumbs-up/up -> THUMBS_UP, thumbs-down/down -> THUMBS_DOWN, love -> LOVE
--rating is optional when --reply-to is present (conditional getFlag vs requireFlag in comments-post.ts lines 29-51)
SKILL.md has Library section with all commands plus complete workflow examples at lines 146-174
Quality gates: npm run build = 'tsc', npm run typecheck = 'tsc --noEmit', npm test = 'tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js'
No new npm dependencies — uses existing http.ts (hxFetch), flags.ts, config.ts
hxFetch uses basePath: '/api' for library endpoints
TypeScript strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 module resolution
unknowns: No library-specific test files exist (flags.test.ts and resolve-ticket.test.ts exist as patterns but no library/*.test.ts)
TypeScript compilation not verified this run
hxFetch error handling for library-specific 404/400 responses not verified
show command markdown heading extraction regex robustness with edge cases (inline code, links in headings) not tested
